Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to merge two logs Lets analyze each log

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Log A has event_type Order Received Order Validated Payment Processed Item Shipped Item Delivered

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Log B has timestamps OrderReceived OrderValidation PaymentCheck Quality Check Shipping

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order Received A vs OrderReceived B Timestamps A 100000 B 095958 Difference 2 seconds Within tolerance 2 seconds So match

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order Validated A vs OrderValidation B A 100130 B 100129 diff 1 sec Match

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Payment Processed A vs PaymentCheck B A 100200 B 100205 diff 5 sec greater than tolerance 2 sec But might still be same

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The spec says If timestamps differ by less than 2 seconds consider them the same event

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we should not match Could treat as separate events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Item Shipped A at 100500 B Shipping at 100502 diff 2 seconds That qualifies as match

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Item Delivered appears only in A not in B So Item Delivered remains unmatched

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus merged events we combine attributes for matched pairs include all attributes from both logs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Use timestamp from Log A primary and include the other timestamp as alternative

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For unmatched events Payment Processed A has no match PaymentCheck B also unmatched since not within tolerance The Quality Check is B only Also Item Delivered is A only

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus final events Order Received both Order Validated both Payment Processed A only PaymentCheck B only Quality Check B only Item Shipped both Item Delivered A only

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now sort chronologically Use primary timestamp from Log A
